libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No Docker image or environment/system property setup is indicated. Tests should target the 2.0.0-M1 contents, primarily org.apache.pekko.macros.LogHelper and LogHelperMacro; avoid stale org.apache.pekko.http.ccompat imports from the old 1.3.0 test because those classes are not present in the 2.0.0-M1 pekko-parsing jar.

deterministic_setup.reason: pekko-parsing_2.13:2.0.0-M1 declares pekko-actor_2.13 as a provided dependency, and LogHelper exposes org.apache.pekko.event.LoggingAdapter types from that artifact.
pekko-parsing_2.13:2.0.0-M1 declares scala-reflect 2.13.18 as a provided dependency, and its macro support classes reference scala.reflect APIs.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8878 [Automation] java run fails for org.apache.pekko:pekko-parsing_2.13:2.0.0-M1; label=fails-java-run; body_chars=8015

risks: The artifact contents are very small and mostly internal macro support, so coverage opportunities may be limited.

Scala macro-oriented tests may still need normal Scala test scaffold support, such as a matching Scala compiler/toolchain configuration, but that is scaffold/test-code work rather than Docker or environment setup.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: The resolved pekko-parsing_2.13:2.0.0-M1 artifact is a small Scala macro/support jar whose POM marks pekko-actor_2.13 and scala-reflect as provided, so meaningful tests that load or exercise LogHelper/LogHelperMacro need those dependencies on the test classpath.
